dialogue.utterance: Dispatch Unit 3 to --Address Redacted-- for a report of a shooting with a downed 11-year-old female victim.
Hi, I'm Lyndee from [CITY] Rescue. What seems to be the issue?
She was shot in the head. She's not responding.
She is combative, actively seizing, and minimally responsive-GCS 8 with eye 2, verbal 2, and motor 4-ePCR.
Eyes deviated to the right, with dilation and sluggish reactivity noted-7-8 mm, equal and sluggish-nystagmus observed-ePCR.
She responds inconsistently with all four limbs-some motor, but flaccid and poor-ePCR confirmed.
Airway is clear with BVM management-respirations are irregular and ineffective-ePCR.
She is placed in the supine position with soft restraints-secured for transport-documented in ePCR.
Respirations are 24, irregular, and deep-sighing-field impression: altered mental status, seizure-ePCR.
Bilateral breath sounds are clear and equal-no adventitious or stridor-ePCR.
SPO2 is 100% via pulse ox-readings remained consistently high-99-100% noted-ePCR.
Brachial pulse is strong, readily palpable 133/min-documented in ePCR.
Skin is dry, warm-no cyanosis documented-ePCR.
Soft upper extremity restraints applied for safety-documented in ePCR.
Initial BP is 109/79-documented in ePCR.
No documented history, medications, or allergies-ePCR confirms unresponsive.
GSW to right posterior head region-extensive brain matter observed around wound-ePCR.
Bullet entered house while victim was sleeping-struck in head-now combative and seizing-ePCR.
Wound site is difficult to visualize-dreadlocks obstruct view-5x9 gauze used but displaced-ePCR.
Pupils are 7-8 mm with nystagmus-sluggish to react-documented in ePCR.
No known ear drainage or obstruction-airway remains patent-documented in ePCR.
Lungs clear, no obstruction-documented as clear and equal in ePCR.
No rigidity or guarding-urinary incontinence observed-no N/V-ePCR.
Limbs visible with variable motor response-documented in ePCR.
Pain is reported at 10-globally combative-documented in ePCR.
BVM initiated for ineffective breathing-field impression noted-ePCR.
Initial EKG is sinus rhythm at 00:45:47-ePCR.
Respiratory rate decreases to 14 and pulse drops to 110-ePCR at 00:50:38.
EKG changes to sinus tachycardia at 00:50:38-ePCR.
PIV in left antecubital-secured after second attempt-documented in ePCR.
Administered 2.5 mg of midazolam to control seizures-improved response-documented in ePCR.
Seizure activity halts-still combative-prior mental status unchanged-ePCR.
NS fluid administered as per protocol-100 ml given en route-documented in ePCR.
Glucose is 0-no further action taken-ePCR.
4-lead monitoring applied per protocol-two instances documented at 00:40:51 and 00:47:54-ePCR.
Patient secured to the stretcher-bundle up-ePCR confirms.
BP increased to 148/128-logged during transport-ePCR.
She's on the stretcher, to be carried to the waiting ambulance-ePCR.
PIV site remains patent-confirmed via aspiration of blood-documented in ePCR.
Patient is fully loaded and secured-documented in ePCR.
Ongoing monitoring with BVM and PIV-tachycardic-ePCR.
Transport destination is [COMPANY_NAME] Trauma Center-Bay #6-ePCR.
She is securely strapped in-ready for en route assessments-ePCR.
Heart rate variable (90s to 150s)-documented in ePCR.
She is on a cardiac monitor-documented on the transport unit-ePCR.
Assumed pediatric care based on age and trauma-documented in ePCR.
Administering 15 LPM oxygen per protocol-documented in ePCR.
No N/V present-documented in ePCR.
Verified pulse 110, BP 148/128, RR 14-ePCR.
5x9 gauze and kling applied to wound site-displaced due to combative state-documented in ePCR.
No additional treatments after 100ml NS-ePCR.
ECG power-on at 00:36-pre-monitoring-documented in ePCR.
Call Type listed as Cardiac Arrest/Death-ePCR.
11-year-old female-documented in ePCR.
Weight 50 kg per documentation-ePCR.
Protocol 10-4 engaged per field impression-epidural hemorrhage suspected-ePCR.
Handoff to trauma and pediatric teams noted-ePCR.
Crew cleared after patient handoff-ePCR.
No post-handoff interventions needed-ePCR.
No infiltration noted at PIV site-documented in ePCR.
Heart rate varies between 90s and 150s-ePCR.
Rechecked Sats via pulse ox-remained at 100%-ePCR.
Sats stable at 100%-documented.
No signature taken-documented in ePCR.
[UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER] and trauma name documented in system-patient's actual name unknown-ePCR.
No personal info obtained-patient is 11-y/o female-documented in ePCR.
4-lead ECG monitoring per protocol (10-4)-documented in ePCR.
Trauma teams were on site at [COMPANY_NAME] in Bay #6-ePCR.
No other major interventions-documented in ePCR.
Patient continuously monitored with BVM and cardiac telemetry-ePCR.
